brent.label: Brent Crude $/bbl
brent.change: +52.4% since Feb 27 — $105-$111 range; Goldman warns >$100 through 2027; IEA: largest supply disruption ever
brent.tooltip: Brent Crude: The international benchmark. Pre-war close: $71.00/bbl (Feb 27). Mar 20 range $105.06-$111.22, settling ~$108.19. Hit $119.11 intraday Mar 19. Goldman Sachs warns oil could stay >$100 through 2027. IEA: 'largest supply disruption in history' — 8+ mbd curtailed. Source: ICE Futures Europe / CNBC / Bloomberg / Goldman Sachs.

wti.label: WTI Crude $/bbl
wti.change: +41.9% since Feb 27 — tracking Brent; 8+ mbd curtailed per IEA; worst case could exceed $147 (2008 high)
wti.tooltip: West Texas Intermediate: The US benchmark. Pre-war close: $66.50/bbl (Feb 27). Trading ~$94.40. IEA: at least 8 mbd curtailed plus 2 mbd condensates/NGLs. Goldman worst case could exceed all-time $147 high from 2008. Source: NYMEX / CNBC / Goldman Sachs.

gas.label: US Gas Avg/Gal
gas.change: +$0.93 / +31.2% since Feb 27 — GA suspends fuel tax — diesel $5.07 (highest since 2022) — CA $5.34-$5.53
gas.tooltip: National average retail gasoline price. Pre-war: $2.98/gal (Feb 27). AAA Mar 20: $3.912/gal (+33% since war began). Diesel $5.07 (highest since 2022). Georgia first state to suspend fuel taxes. CA $5.34-$5.53. Jones Act waived Mar 18. Source: AAA / EIA / CNN.

sp500.label: S&P 500
sp500.change: -284 pts / -4.1% — new 2026 low below 200-day MA; Dow ~45,973; VIX 25.80; 4th weekly loss
sp500.tooltip: S&P 500 index. Pre-war close: 6,878.88 (Feb 27). New 2026 closing low ~6,595 on Quadruple Witching Friday — first close below 200-day MA in 214 sessions. 4th consecutive weekly loss. VIX surged to 25.80 intraday. Some traders pricing possible 2026 rate hike. 10-yr yield at 6.75-month high of 4.32%. Source: NYSE / CNBC / FRED.

daily_cost.label: Est. Daily US Cost
daily_cost.note: Record tempo continues — 7,800+ cumulative targets — $200B supplemental requested
daily_cost.tooltip: Estimated daily operational cost. Day 21: CENTCOM confirmed 7,800+ targets struck cumulative. IDF struck 130+ targets in 24 hrs including first Caspian Sea strikes. Pentagon requesting $200B supplemental. Source: CENTCOM / Pentagon briefing / CSIS / Penn Wharton.

total_cost.label: Est. Total Cost (21 Days)
total_cost.note: Pentagon $11.3B Day 6 + CSIS $16.5B Day 12 + $200B supplemental requested
total_cost.tooltip: Cumulative estimated cost since Feb 28. Pentagon: $11.3B through Day 6. CSIS: $16.5B through Day 12. Extrapolated through Day 21 at ~$900M/day. Does not include VA costs or economic impact ($50-210B per Penn Wharton). Source: Pentagon / CSIS / Penn Wharton.

targets_struck.label: Targets Struck
targets_struck.note: CENTCOM: 7,800+ US — 120+ vessels destroyed — IDF 130+ in 24 hrs — ~22,000+ combined est.
targets_struck.tooltip: Total targets struck by US and Israeli forces since Feb 28. CENTCOM confirmed 7,800+ US targets by Mar 20. IDF struck 130+ regime infrastructure targets in 24 hrs including first Caspian Sea strikes. 120+ Iranian naval vessels and 44 minelayers destroyed. Combined US/Israeli extrapolated ~22,000+. Source: CENTCOM / IDF / Hegseth briefing.

us_kia.label: US Military Deaths
us_kia.note: 13 KIA confirmed + 1 non-hostile death — F-35 struck by possible AA fire Mar 19
us_kia.tooltip: US military deaths. 6 at Port Shuaiba, Kuwait (Day 2). 1 died of wounds at Prince Sultan AB, Saudi Arabia (Day 9). 6 killed in KC-135 crash over Iraq (Day 13). 1 non-hostile death in Kuwait. F-35 emergency landing Mar 19 — pilot stable — CENTCOM investigating possible hostile fire (first US jet struck if confirmed). Source: DoD / CENTCOM / CNN.

us_wia.label: US Service Members WIA
us_wia.note: 140+ total — F-35 pilot in stable condition after emergency landing
us_wia.tooltip: US service members wounded/injured across all theaters since Feb 28. 140+ total. Includes casualties at Port Shuaiba, Prince Sultan AB, mine countermeasures, and base attacks. F-35 pilot in stable condition after emergency landing Mar 19. Source: DoD / CENTCOM.

iranian_killed.label: Iranian Killed (Hengaw est.)
iranian_killed.note: Hengaw: 5,300 (511 civilian) — Al Jazeera: 1,444 — HRANA: 3,154 — Red Crescent: 204 children
iranian_killed.tooltip: Iranian casualties. Al Jazeera tracker (Mar 20): 1,444 killed including 204 children per Iranian Red Crescent, 18,000+ injured. Hengaw (5th report, Mar 18): 5,300 killed — 511 civilians (120 minors, 160 women), 4,800+ military. HRANA (Mar 17): ~3,154 deaths. 9 hospitals non-operational. Source: Al Jazeera / Hengaw / HRANA / Iranian Red Crescent.

lebanese_killed.label: Lebanese Killed
lebanese_killed.note: 1,001+ killed, 2,584+ wounded — 1M+ displaced — 118+ children — 570+ Hezbollah operatives eliminated
lebanese_killed.tooltip: Lebanese casualties from Israeli strikes and IDF ground operations since March 2. 1,001+ killed including 118+ children, 2,584+ wounded, 1M+ displaced (~1/5 of population). IDF struck 2,000+ targets in Lebanon, eliminated 570+ operatives including 220 Radwan Force members. Hezbollah: 668 cumulative attack waves (daily avg 37, trending up). Source: Lebanese Health Ministry / OCHA / IDF.

displaced.label: Displaced Persons
displaced.note: Iran: up to 3.2M + Lebanon: 1M+ — internet at 4% — UNHCR / OCHA
displaced.tooltip: People displaced from their homes across all fronts. Iran: 600,000-1M households (up to 3.2M people). Lebanon: 1M+. Internet at 4% of normal. 25+ hospitals damaged, 9 forced out of service. Source: UNHCR / OCHA / NetBlocks.

flights_cancelled.label: Flights Cancelled
flights_cancelled.note: 24,000+ flights — 3,200 vessels stranded — 20+ vessel attacks — 15,000 cruise passengers
flights_cancelled.tooltip: Commercial flights cancelled due to airspace closures. IMO: 3,200 vessels, 20,000-40,000 seafarers stranded. 15,000 cruise passengers stranded on 6+ ships. ~400 vessels in Gulf of Oman backlog. 20+ confirmed maritime incidents. 44 GPS injection zones + 92 denial areas. Jones Act waived. Source: IATA / Flightradar24 / IMO / Bloomberg.

children_killed.label: Children Killed
children_killed.note: Iran: 204 (Red Crescent) inc. ~168 Minab — Lebanon: 118+ — OHCHR
children_killed.tooltip: Children killed in the conflict. Iranian Red Crescent (Mar 20): 204 children killed including ~168 at Minab girls' school (Day 1). Lebanon: 118+ children among 1,001+ total dead. Amnesty confirmed US responsible for Minab via Tomahawk missile. Source: Iranian Red Crescent / Amnesty / Lebanese Health Ministry / OHCHR.
